relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: He questions Job’s claim about justice and righteousness.
He hears Job asking what good righteousness does.
Elihu answers Job and the friends together.
He points Job upward to heavens and clouds.
Human wrongdoing does not injure God as it injures humans.
Human righteousness does not enrich God as though God lacked something.
Human harm and righteousness touch other humans.
The oppressed cry out under great arms of power.
Yet Elihu says people often fail to seek the Maker who gives songs in the night.
The Maker teaches humans beyond beasts and birds.
Cries may go unanswered where pride rules.
Elihu says empty crying is not regarded by Shaddai.
He tells Job to wait with the case before God.
He argues that delayed judgment has made Job speak boldly.
He closes by saying Job multiplies words without knowledge.
